title: sphaeralcea subhastata var. martii
intro: Sphaeralcea subhastata var. martii, commonly known as Martinez's globemallow, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It belongs to the mallow family and is known for its bright orange to yellow flowers. Martinez's globemallow has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The plant is used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for consumption.
